reasoning: Bobby Finke set the 1500m freestyle long course world record (14:30.67) at the Paris Olympics in August 2024, breaking a 12-year-old record held by Sun Yang (14:31.02). The 1500m freestyle record is historically extremely durable; prior to Sun Yang, Grant Hackett's record lasted for 10 years. For Finke to lose the record before early 2026, another swimmer would have to surpass 14:30.67 (if Finke breaks his own record, he remains the record holder). The closest current competitor is Daniel Wiffen, whose personal best is roughly 3.4 seconds slower. Furthermore, long course records are typically only set at major international meets. The only such meet before the resolution date is the 2025 World Championships in Singapore. It is highly unlikely that another swimmer will close the gap and break this newly set record within the next year and a half. Therefore, the probability that Finke continues to hold the record is very high.
